brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 115 (C) with all three time steps correctly computed; goes one level beyond calculation to explain the ecological mechanism (predator suppression failure → prey overshoot) but does not trace second-order effects like oscillation period or stability; proceeds linearly through the problem with no reflection on assumptions, rounding decisions, or potential calculation errors; shows all intermediate values but performs no cross-check (e.g., re-deriving from continuous model or sanity-checking via equilibrium proximity); presents the answer as certain with no acknowledgment that discrete LV can exhibit chaotic behavior at some parameter regimes or that rounding at each step affects precision; considers only direct simulation without mentioning analytical linearization or matrix-eigenvalue alternatives; every arithmetic step is fully written out with explicit substitution, making the chain entirely reproducible by a third party.
